title: Scaffold Node/TypeScript project
description: package.json, tsconfig, build/test/lint tooling, dir layout. Root dependency for all code. Target stack per SPEC §3.2: Node/TS, Agent SDK, Slack Bolt, SQLite, stdio MCP.
close_reason: Scaffold complete: pnpm+Vitest+Biome, ESM, Node22, TS 5.9.3 strict/NodeNext, dir skeleton, smoke test green. Commits 612e715 (scaffold) + ef1c356 (pinning fixes). Review clean after fix loop.

title: Project Foundation & Runtime
description: Greenfield scaffold and the daemon runtime host. Owns: Node/TypeScript project setup (build/test/lint), Claude Agent SDK harness (stdio MCP), config system (pinned week-boundary TZ + trigger time, per-model rates, cook-night counts), secrets loading (1Password service account primary, chmod-600 env fallback), dev/prod profile switch (channel ID + separate SQLite path + force-regenerate + post/dry-run), and the launchd daemon plist (boot-launch + KeepAlive) plus the in-process Sunday scheduler. Root dependency for all other epics. Source: SPEC §3.2, §7, §9.1, §9.4.
close_reason: Project Foundation & Runtime complete: all 7 tasks done (scaffold, config, Agent SDK harness, dev/prod profile, secrets, daemon+scheduler, harness hardening). Reviewed via SDD; 79 tests green on build/phase-1-foundation.

title: DECISION: 1Password service-account availability for boot secrets
description: SPEC §9.1 / §10.3 open item. Confirm the op CLI / 1Password supports service accounts readable at daemon boot (no interactive unlock), else fold setup into the build or use the env-file fallback. EXTERNAL LEAD TIME — start early. Gates secrets loading (T1 secrets). Ratify: bd close --reason.
design:
RECOMMENDATION: 1Password service account as primary boot-secret source; chmod-600 env-file fallback sanctioned. VERIFIED against official docs (developer.1password.com → 1password.dev, fetched 2026-07-11):

- Fully non-interactive: setting OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN authenticates the `op` CLI with NO interactive unlock — exactly the launchd-at-boot path this needs. `op read op://vault/item/field` works with the token alone. CONFIRMS the SPEC §9.1 assumption.
- Rate limits: service accounts have hourly/daily request caps. One `op read` = 3 requests by default; pass explicit vault + item IDs to cut it to 1. v1.0 reads 2 secrets at startup → trivial (≤6 req/boot, or 2 with IDs). Non-issue.
- Caveat: OP_CONNECT_HOST / OP_CONNECT_TOKEN take precedence over OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN — ensure they are unset in the daemon env.
- Residual boot caveat (already noted in SPEC §9.1): the SA token itself must be readable at boot. This does not eliminate the root-secret problem, it narrows it to one rotatable/auditable token — provide it via the launchd plist EnvironmentVariables or a chmod-600 file the daemon reads at startup.

NET: no blocker to making 1Password SA primary. Implementation notes for T1 secrets: use vault+item IDs in `op read` calls; clear OP_CONNECT_* in the daemon env; provision OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN outside any repo. Env-file fallback remains available (v1.0 needs only Slack bot token + Anthropic key).

To ratify: bd close meal-planner-njt --reason "..."  (auto-unblocks T1 secrets once scaffold is also done).
close_reason: Ratified (fact verified against official 1Password docs 2026-07-11): 1Password service account is the primary boot-secret source — OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN gives fully non-interactive op-CLI auth suitable for a launchd daemon at boot; rate limits are a non-issue for 2 startup secrets. chmod-600 env-file fallback remains sanctioned. Implementation notes captured in the decision design (use vault+item IDs; clear OP_CONNECT_*; provision token outside any repo).

title: DECISION: week_key format (Sunday-date vs ISO year-week)
description: ADR 0002 D1 open item. week_key must be pinned as either the anchor Sunday's date or ISO year-week. Gates the week_key computation (T3 week_key) and the session PRIMARY KEY. Ratify: bd close --reason.
design: RECOMMENDATION: anchor Sunday's date (e.g. '2026-07-12') in the pinned TZ. Rationale (ADR 0002 D1): dodges ISO's Monday/Sunday boundary confusion given a Sunday trigger; human-readable; sorts chronologically. Depends on the pinned TZ + trigger time config (T1 config). Ratify by closing.
close_reason: Ratified: week_key = anchor Sunday's date in the pinned TZ (e.g. '2026-07-12'), per ADR 0002 D1 — dodges ISO Monday/Sunday boundary confusion given the Sunday trigger, human-readable, sorts chronologically. Used as the session PRIMARY KEY. Depends on pinned TZ + trigger time (T1 config).

title: Per-run generation hard cap (~$2)
description: In-code hard cap ~$2 (~500K tokens) on a generation run — Anthropic offers alerts, not enforced cutoffs, so the orchestrator enforces its own. On cap hit: stop, alert #agent-alerts, never die silently. SPEC §9.3.

title: Token/$ tracking across a run
description: Accumulate tokens (returned per response) across all Agent SDK loop calls within one run; convert to $ via per-model rates in config; persist to session counters (token_spend/cost_usd). A run aggregates across all calls, not per-call. SPEC §9.3.

title: Post draft + capture thread ts
description: chat.postMessage to the profile's channel ID as a new top-level message (thread-per-week); capture parent thread ts (load-bearing). Called by generateForWeek between the 'generating' insert and the 'suggested' update. SPEC §7.

title: Create Slack app + channels (bot token, chat:write)
description: New Slack app in Backhaushold workspace; bot token (xoxb) with chat:write ONLY — no Socket Mode / app-level token in v1.0. Channels: #meal-plan, #dev-meal-plan, #agent-alerts (#grocery-list is v4.0). SPEC §7, §9.2.

title: WeekPlan → Slack markdown render
description: Deterministic render of WeekPlan to Slack markdown (selection≠presentation, ADR 0003 D3): surface per-meal veg path, do-ahead/untested flags, rationale, summary. LLM never writes the post. ADR 0003 D3.

title: Post-validation + one bounded repair retry
description: validate(): counts match slots, every recipe_id (incl second_dish) a real pool member, veg path present+consistent (inherent/second_dish checked vs veg_status; separable accepted on model word v1.0), no duplicates, flag sanity. On fail: ONE repair re-prompt with violations, else throw → alert+fail run. ADR 0003 D5.

title: WeekPlan schema + single LLM selection call
description: WeekPlan structured output (meals[]: slot_type, recipe_id, title, day=null v1.0, veg VegPath, flags, rationale) == working_plan. Single llmSelect over cheap-tier candidates; LLM decides, no agentic loop. ADR 0003 D1/D3/D4.

title: PlannerInput assembly + prompt builder
description: Assemble PlannerInput (slots, pools, household prose incl. veg daughter HARD + picky youngest, current_season, untested_present) and build the selection prompt (TASK/HOUSEHOLD/SLOTS/CANDIDATES/RULES/OUTPUT). Signals by mechanism (D2). ADR 0003.

title: Deterministic pool composition
description: buildPlan step 1: weeknight pool (active_max:60, limit=constrained*4) + weekend pool (relaxed, limit=relaxed*4); ensureVegFloor top-up via veg_status:vegetarian; maybeInjectUntested at configured rate. All code-side policy, no LLM. ADR 0003 D1, §6.4.

title: Startup catch-up (three cases)
description: onStartup: no row past trigger → generate; live row (suggested/under_revision/committed) → resume; stale 'generating' → alert ONCE to #agent-alerts, set failed, never auto-repost/auto-generate. ADR 0002 D4.

title: week_key computation (clock-derived)
description: currentPlanWeek(now,cfg): most-recent-Sunday-on-or-before in pinned TZ, gated on trigger time; previousPlanWeek; triggerMoment. 'Active week' is computed, never stored (ADR 0002 D3). Depends on config TZ/trigger.

title: State machine + generateForWeek (idempotency + write-before-post)
description: State machine generating→suggested→under_revision→committed +failed +expired. generateForWeek: idempotency gate (skip if row exists unless force), expirePriorIfUncommitted piggyback, insert 'generating' BEFORE post, update 'suggested'+thread_ts+working_plan AFTER ts. ADR 0002 D1/D2.

title: SQLite session schema + migrations
description: session table keyed by week_key (status, thread_ts nullable, working_plan JSON with nullable per-meal day, turn_count, token_spend, cost_usd, timestamps) + idx_session_thread_ts. Cost counters colocated so §9.3 caps update transactionally. ADR 0002.

title: search_recipes tool (cheap fan-out)
description: Vector search over query + deterministic filter predicates: active_max (HARD; null/low-confidence time FAILS → fail-closed), veg_status, season, effort, exclude_ids (defined now, used v2.0), limit. Returns lightweight RecipeCandidate (NO ingredient block). ADR 0001 D2/D3.
close_reason: search_recipes complete: vector search + deterministic post-filters (fail-closed active_max w/ confidence threshold, veg_status/season/effort), lightweight RecipeCandidate (no ingredients), exclude_ids passthrough, over-fetch to refill limit. Commit 755d081. Review Approved (minor untested branch combos only).

title: Ingest-time extraction pass (hash + version gated)
description: Single LLM pass per note → {time{active,total,prep,confidence}, ingredients[], veg_status, effort_tags, season_tags, quality}. Cache keyed by note.id, gated on contentHash AND extractor_version (re-tuning forces clean re-extract). Runs at sync, not per plan-run. ADR 0001 D1.
notes: From schema review (q95.1): schema.ts zod objects are NOT .strict() (unlike config.ts), so unrecognized keys are silently stripped. When validating LLM extraction output here, consider .strict() (or an explicit allowlist) to catch hallucinated/drifted fields early rather than dropping them silently.
close_reason: Ingest-time extraction pass complete: extraction.ts (LLM→ExtractedFields, strict validation, 1 bounded repair retry) + structured-store.ts (note_id cache, hash+extractor_version gated) + sync wiring with per-note error isolation. Commit 9e1767b. Review Approved (Moderate/Minor findings → follow-up filed).

title: Apple Notes MCP source + vector index
description: Apple Notes MCP + harness; sync notes and build/refresh the local vector index. Owns index freshness. ADR 0001 D1.
close_reason: Implemented notes-reader (osascript/JXA), transformers.js embedder, sqlite-vec vector store, and hash-gated sync. 147 tests pass; typecheck/lint/build clean.

title: launchd daemon + in-process Sunday scheduler
description: launchd plist = boot-launch + KeepAlive only (no calendar job). In-process scheduler fires generation at the Sunday slot. Confirm system sleep disabled (pmset -g → sleep 0); do one real test-fire of the scheduler path. SPEC §3.2, §9.4.
notes: From secrets review (7js.3): the op-CLI execFile calls have no timeout — a hung op at boot would hang the daemon indefinitely. When wiring boot-time secret loading in this task, add a timeout (execFile {timeout} or Promise race) with a clear failure/alert path.
close_reason: Daemon + in-process scheduler complete: DST-correct croner scheduler with error containment, injected onTrigger/onStartup seams for E3, boot secret-load timeout, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY wiring, pmset sleep-check, launchd plist. Commits d8d1dbe + 160286c. Review Approved after fix loop (Important+Medium fixed).

title: Freeze ingredient + shared value-type schema
description: Frozen capture schema (ADR 0001 D4): Ingredient{raw always, name, prep separate, quantity(scalar|range|none), unit nullable, optional, alternatives, group, confidence, needs_review} + shared types Quantity/VegStatus/Quality/TimeFields. Capture≠aggregation; raw always kept.
close_reason: Frozen recipe schema complete: shared value types + Ingredient + two-tier RecipeCandidate/Recipe as zod schemas with z.infer TS types (single source of truth). Exact ADR 0001 fidelity, no package_size (ratified→raw). Commit 809d622. Review Approved (Minor nits only).

title: Claude Agent SDK harness
description: Wire the Agent SDK to drive Claude over local MCP (stdio). Model = claude-sonnet-5 medium effort, as per-context config (not hardcoded). Gotchas: no manual budget_tokens (use effort), no non-default temperature/top_p/top_k, tokenizer ~1.0-1.35x. SPEC §9.3, §3.2.
close_reason: Agent SDK harness complete: LlmClient seam (only src/llm imports the SDK), Sonnet-5 gotchas enforced via real assertions, usage aggregation across turns, SDK shape verified vs installed .d.ts. Commit 42f09c9. Review Approved.

title: dev/prod profile switch
description: Single --profile dev|prod bundles: target channel ID (explicit, never name lookup), separate SQLite path per profile, force-regenerate default (on in dev), post-vs-dry-run. SPEC §7.
close_reason: dev/prod profile switch complete: resolveProfile bundles channelId/sqlitePath/forceRegenerate/postMode with SPEC §7 isolation enforced (no cross-profile fallback, #name rejection, distinct sqlite paths, collision throws). Matches config.ts validation style. Commit fa631a7. Review Approved (Minor nits only).

title: Config system + boot validation
description: Load and validate config: pinned week-boundary TZ + trigger time (load-bearing for week_key — ADR 0002), per-model $ rates, cook-night counts (constrained/relaxed). Validate at boot; a wrong/unset TZ silently mis-keys weeks. SPEC §9.4, ADR 0002.
close_reason: Config system + boot validation complete: typed zod schema, strict IANA tz validation (Intl.DateTimeFormat), HH:MM trigger, exact SPEC §9.3 defaults, error aggregation. Commit 7e10ea3. Review Approved (Minor nits only, tracked for final review).

title: Secrets loading (1Password SA / env fallback)
description: 1Password service account primary (fetch at startup; daemon can't do interactive unlock at boot), chmod-600 env-file fallback. v1.0 needs only Slack bot token (xoxb) + Anthropic API key. SPEC §9.1.
close_reason: Secrets loading complete: 1Password SA (op read, OP_CONNECT_* cleared unconditionally) + env fallback, only the 2 v1.0 secrets, no-leak enforced (never reads rejection reason; adversarial test) + execFile args array. Commit b71640e. Review Approved (no Critical/High).

title: Planner
description: Hybrid selection (ADR 0003): the code guarantees, the LLM decides. Owns: deterministic pool composition (weeknight active_max pool + weekend pool, veg-floor top-up, untested injection — all code-side policy), a single LLM selection call over cheap-tier candidates with signals-by-mechanism (structured fields as data, household context + kid-friendliness as prose), the WeekPlan structured output (== working_plan; per-meal veg path inherent/separable/second_dish; day always null in v1.0), deterministic post-validation (counts, pool membership, veg consistency, no duplicates, flag sanity) with ONE bounded repair retry, and get_recipe enrich of the chosen. Depends on E2. Source: ADR 0003, SPEC §6.

title: Slack Posting & Draft Rendering
description: Outbound-only Slack for v1.0 (SPEC §7, §9.2). Owns: the new Slack app in the Backhaushold workspace (bot token, chat:write scope only — NO Socket Mode / app-level token in v1.0), posting the weekly draft via chat.postMessage as a new top-level message (thread-per-week), a deterministic WeekPlan→Slack-markdown render step (selection ≠ presentation, ADR 0003 D3), and capturing/persisting the parent thread ts (load-bearing for v3.0). Target channel resolved by explicit ID from the dev/prod profile (never a name lookup). Called by E3's generateForWeek. Source: SPEC §7, §9.2, ADR 0003 D3.

title: Orchestrator: State, Idempotency & Recovery
description: The daemon's core loop and durable state (ADR 0002). Owns: SQLite session schema (keyed by week_key), the state machine (generating→suggested→under_revision→committed, +failed +expired), week_key computation (currentPlanWeek: clock-derived anchor Sunday in pinned TZ), the generation idempotency gate, write-before-post ordering (insert 'generating' BEFORE Slack post; update to 'suggested'+thread_ts+working_plan AFTER ts returns), startup catch-up (three cases; stale 'generating' → alert-once-then-failed, never auto-repost), expired piggybacked on next generation, and the cost-counter columns (turn_count/token_spend/cost_usd). Calls E4 (buildPlan) then E5 (post). Source: ADR 0002, SPEC §8.

title: Recipe MCP Server
description: Custom Apple Notes MCP server + harness that we own (ADR 0001). Owns: local vector index, an ingest-time LLM extraction pass gated on content-hash + extractor_version, a cached structured-field store, the frozen ingredient capture schema (raw always kept; name/prep/quantity(range|none)/unit/optional/alternatives/group/confidence), and the two-tier tool interface — search_recipes (cheap fan-out; hard predicates active_max + veg_status, fail-closed on null/low-confidence time) and get_recipe (full incl. ingredient block, chosen only). Consumed by the Planner (E4). Ingredient block built in v1.0, fully consumed in v4.0. Source: ADR 0001, SPEC §5.

title: Cap/backoff for permanently-failing note extraction + tighten sync failure handling
description: Follow-up from extraction review (q95.3, commit 9e1767b). (1) MODERATE: a note whose extraction deterministically fails re-attempts (1 initial + 1 repair = 2 LLM calls) on EVERY sync forever, no backoff/cap — because needs_review is a staleness OR-condition and the failed record rewrites content_hash to current. Add a per-note attempt cap or backoff (e.g. stop retrying after N consecutive failures until the body changes) to bound cost/noise. Does NOT cascade into the embed gate (independence holds). (2) MINOR: sync.ts failure try/catch wraps both extractRecipeFields AND the success-path upsertStructured — a DB write error after a SUCCESSFUL extraction is mislabeled as an extraction failure, discarding a good result; tighten the try to just the extraction call. (3) MINOR: the catch discards the error entirely — log a redacted reason (error.message is already proven secret-free) for triage. Non-blocking; extraction runs ~weekly.

title: Surface per-turn LLM error messages in the SDK harness
description: Follow-up from the Agent SDK harness review (7js.5, commit 42f09c9). The adapter only throws on a non-success FINAL result message; per-turn SDKAssistantMessage.error values (authentication_failed, billing_error, rate_limit, overloaded, server_error) are not inspected — a turn could error yet still contribute usage and let the loop reach a 'success' result, producing silent partial failures that are hard to debug. Inspect and surface per-turn errors (throw or propagate) before E2 extraction and E4 selection consume the harness. Also (nits from same review): drop the unnecessary optional-usage guard, and include SDKResultError.errors[] in the thrown message instead of just the subtype string.
close_reason: Per-turn SDKAssistantMessage.error now throws immediately (first-error-wins, named error type, no secrets); non-success final result includes SDKResultError.errors[]; dead usage guard dropped. TDD, all 4 gates green. Commit 481cd7f on build/phase-1-foundation.

title: DECISION: planner tuning params (vegFloorK, untestedRate, time-penalty)
description: ADR 0003 open item. Concrete values for vegFloorK, untestedRate, and total-time penalty weighting. DATA-DEPENDENT: tuned during the plan-quality validation pass (T4 tune) on the real corpus; code ships with config defaults until then. Gates T4 tune.

title: DECISION: seed-query strategy (single vs category-seeded multi-query)
description: ADR 0003 open item. Pool diversity: a single generic diversity seed vs category/cuisine-seeded multi-query. DATA-DEPENDENT: pick only if v1.0 pools come back homogeneous (a tuning knob, not a redesign). Gates T4 tune.

title: DECISION: operator re-run affordance for a failed week
description: ADR 0002 open item. When a week goes 'failed' (mid-flight crash), define the manual re-run mechanism: a --force regenerate flag, delete-the-row, or a small 're-run week W' command. Gates the re-run task (T3 rerun). Ratify: bd close --reason.
design: RECOMMENDATION: a 'bd-style' CLI subcommand on the daemon, e.g. 're-run <week_key> [--force]', that clears/overwrites the failed row and invokes generateForWeek with force=true. Reuses the dev force-regenerate path (T1 profile). Avoids raw row-deletion (error-prone) and keeps a clean audit. Ratify by closing.
close_reason: Ratified: failed-week recovery is a daemon CLI subcommand 're-run <week_key> [--force]' that clears/overwrites the failed row and calls generateForWeek(force=true), reusing the dev force-regenerate path (T1 profile). Avoids raw row-deletion; keeps a clean audit. Per ADR 0002 open item.

title: DECISION: active_max confidence threshold (fail-closed rule)
description: ADR 0001 open item. Pick the concrete time.confidence value below which a candidate FAILS active_max (fail-closed → not weeknight-eligible). DATA-DEPENDENT: resolved during the extraction-quality validation pass (T2 valid) against the real corpus — do not guess now. Gates T2 search + T2 valid.

title: DECISION: ingredient package_size capture (structure vs raw)
description: ADR 0001 open item / SPEC §5.1. count-with-package-size ('1 can (14 oz)', '2 (400 g) tins') — add a structured package_size field or leave in the raw line for v4.0 to parse. Gates the frozen schema (T2 schema) + extraction (T2 extract). Ratify: bd close --reason.
design: RECOMMENDATION: leave in raw for v1.0 (the doc's default when undecided). raw is always retained losslessly, so nothing is lost and v4.0 can parse or retrofit a structured field then — package_size is a v4.0 aggregation concern, not consumed in v1.0. Revisit at v4.0 grocery aggregation. Ratify by closing.
close_reason: Ratified: leave count-with-package-size in the raw ingredient line for v1.0 (ADR 0001 / SPEC §5.1 default). raw is retained losslessly, so v4.0 grocery aggregation can parse it or retrofit a structured package_size field then. Not consumed in v1.0.

title: Own API workspace/key + low-spend email alert
description: New Anthropic API key in its own workspace (independent visibility/revocability); metered per-token (subscription cap does NOT catch this). Low-spend email alert at a threshold well below alarming (a few $/month is a genuine anomaly). SPEC §9.3.

title: #agent-alerts wiring + local log
description: #agent-alerts is alerts-only (no heartbeat, no 'skipping this week'). Complete local log on disk. SPEC §9.4.

title: Plan-quality validation & parameter tuning
description: On the real corpus, tune vegFloorK, untestedRate, total-time penalty weighting, and seed-query strategy (single generic vs category-seeded multi-query if pools look homogeneous). ADR 0003 open items.

title: Enrich chosen via get_recipe
description: get_recipe(id) for the ~5-6 chosen (source links in v1.0; ingredient block load-bearing in v4.0). ADR 0003 pipeline step 4.

title: Operator re-run affordance for failed/skipped week
description: A mechanism to manually re-run a week that went failed (mid-flight crash) — gated on the re-run-affordance decision. ADR 0002 open items.

title: Resume-quietly on restart
description: Reload working_plan + thread_ts from SQLite and continue; say nothing in-thread (v3.0 listening). ADR 0002 / SPEC §8.

title: Extraction-quality validation on real corpus
description: Run the extraction pass on the actual recipe corpus; eyeball confidence/needs_review; pick the concrete active_max confidence threshold for the fail-closed rule. Build-time validation, not a design unknown. ADR 0001 consequences/open items.

title: get_recipe tool (full; chosen only)
description: Return full Recipe = RecipeCandidate + ingredients[] (frozen schema) + body + source_note_id. Called only for the ~5-6 chosen. ADR 0001 D2.
close_reason: get_recipe complete: full Recipe assembly (note meta + structured fields incl. ingredient block), RecipeSchema-validated, null for unknown/not-ready. Added VectorStore.getNote getter. Commit 92218b7. Review Approved (nits only).

title: Cost Control & Ops/Alerting
description: Cost enforcement and operability (SPEC §9.3, §9.4). Owns: in-code dollar-denominated cost tracking (tokens returned per response → cost via per-model rates in config), the per-run generation hard cap (~$2 / ~500K tokens; trips only on a true runaway), the Anthropic low-spend email alert, the #agent-alerts channel wiring (alerts-only: no heartbeat), and a complete local log on disk. Turn/thread caps are v3.0 but the tracking infra + generation cap are v1.0. Hooks into E3 (counters on session row) and E4 (generation token tracking). Source: SPEC §9.3, §9.4.
